01

Palm Beach Parking Permits 2026

Town of Palm Beach — Permit Types, Costs & Application Process

Below is a full overview of how to obtain parking permits for the Town of Palm Beach for 2026, including permit types, eligibility, costs, required documents, and step-by-step application instructions.

Clarify What's Needed Before Taking Any Action

Before beginning any application, I would confirm with the principal:

The property address on the island (determines eligibility: RPP vs. PPP)
The number of vehicles that need permits (maximum 2 per household)
Whether visitor hangtags are needed and for how many guests (up to 4 per RPP household)
Any commercial or staff vehicles that may require a Parking Placard Permit

This prevents wasted effort and ensures the right permit type is applied for from the start.

Permit Types & Costs

Permit Type Who It's For Cost Key Benefit
Resident Parking Permit (RPP) Residents on qualifying street blocks $50/vehicle Park on your designated block; up to 48 hrs
ParkMobile Parking Permit (PPP) All Town residents $50/vehicle 2 free hours in any ParkMobile zone town-wide
RPP Visitor Hangtag Guests of RPP-eligible residents $50/hangtag Park on resident's RPP block (up to 4 per household)
Parking Placard Permit Employers / commercial users $165/mo · $554/4 mo · $777/6 mo · $1,454/yr Unlimited parking in designated commercial areas; waitlist may apply
Key Rules
Maximum of 2 permits per household for both RPP and PPP. All resident permits are valid through December 31, 2026. RPP residents may add PPP for an additional $50, or purchase PPP on its own.

Documents to Gather Before Applying

Proof of Residency (one required)
Current utility bill; court-registered mortgage document, deed, condominium contract, or trust document; current Florida ID or Driver's License showing Town address; or current lease agreement (must include landlord's name and contact number). Note: hotels and short-term rentals under 12 months are not eligible.
Vehicle Information (all required)
Make and model; license plate number (vehicle does not need a Florida plate).
Supporting Vehicle Document (one required)
Car registration, insurance card, or rental contract.

How to Apply — Online (Recommended)

The Town uses the Gtechna parking permit portal, which launched for 2026 registrations on January 26, 2026.

1
Create an account

Navigate to the Palm Beach Permit Portal, click "Sign Up" in the top right. The system automatically determines your eligibility (RPP or PPP) based on your address.

2
Register your vehicle

Click "Add Permit," then "Add Vehicle," enter vehicle details, and upload your Vehicle Registration and Proof of Residency.

3
Select your permit type

RPP residents: location defaults to your block. PPP residents: select "Townwide ParkMobile Parking Permit" from the dropdown.

4
Add visitor hangtags (optional — RPP residents only)

Click "Add Permit," then "Add Vehicle," toggle "Is this a visitor vehicle?" to Yes. Use format: VIS + last 5 digits of your phone number + permit number (e.g., VIS434561).

5
Pay and submit

Click "Add Credit Card," enter card details, accept terms, and click "Buy." Your card is not charged until the application is approved. Confirmation arrives by email.

Resources

▶ Watch Step-by-Step Video Tutorial ↓ Download Registration Instructions (PDF)

How to Apply — In Person

Visit Town Hall at 360 South County Road, Palm Beach, FL 33480, Monday through Friday, 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM.

Important Details

A credit card is required to complete your application, but payment will only be processed once your application is approved.
Approved permits will be confirmed via email.
We're here to help! If you are having issues, please fill out the contact form.

02

4-Day Mallorca Itinerary

Summer 2026 — No Flights Included

Palma de Mallorca is recommended as the base for all four days. It's centrally located, has the best hotel and restaurant concentration on the island, and all four destinations are within a 45 to 75-minute drive.

Day 1 Palma de Mallorca

Start at the Gothic Cathedral La Seu on the waterfront — allow at least an hour. Walk through the old town to the Almudaina Royal Palace, then through the Arab Baths neighborhood and the La Llotja district. End the afternoon along the Passeig Maritim promenade. Dinner in the Santa Catalina neighborhood, which has the best concentration of restaurants in the city.

Day 2 Serra de Tramuntana

UNESCO World Heritage mountain range. First stop Valldemossa, a hilltop village built around a 14th-century monastery. Continue to Deià, a small artists' village with dramatic cliffside views and a rocky cove below. End in Sóller — a preserved Art Nouveau town in an orange grove valley. Take the vintage wooden tram down to Port de Sóller for dinner by the harbor.

Day 3 Northern Coast

Start in Pollença — walk the 365-step Calvari staircase for views over the bay. Continue to Cap de Formentor, the island's northernmost point: a dramatic limestone peninsula with cliffs dropping into turquoise water. The drive itself is the experience. Return via the walled Roman town of Alcúdia and walk the intact medieval walls before dinner in the old town.

Day 4 Southeast Coast

The least developed stretch of coast on the island. Drive to Es Trenc — a long natural beach with Caribbean-quality water and no resort infrastructure behind it (protected land). Stop in Santanyí on the way, which has a good Saturday artisan market if timing aligns. Optional: a half-day boat excursion to Cabrera National Park, a protected island reserve approximately 10 km offshore, bookable from Colonia de Sant Jordi.

03

Online Survey

Microsoft Forms + Power BI — 150 to 200 Respondents

The recommended platform is Microsoft Forms, which integrates natively with the M365 ecosystem. Responses aggregate automatically into a live Excel workbook stored in OneDrive. A connected Power BI dashboard pulls from that workbook and delivers a clean one-page visual summary — response counts, averages, and distribution charts — that updates in real time as submissions come in.

A scheduled Power BI subscription can be configured to email the one-page summary to any stakeholder on a daily cadence during the collection window, requiring no manual export or reporting work.

Tool Stack
Microsoft Forms (collection) → Excel on OneDrive (live data layer) → Power BI (one-page summary dashboard) → Power BI Subscription (automated daily email delivery)

The full form will be built and linked here once the question document is received.

04

Consolidating Outlook Contacts

Multiple Lists, 1,000+ Contacts Each — No Duplicates

With 1,000-plus contacts per list, the most reliable approach is a structured export, merge, and re-import using Excel as the intermediary. All original lists remain intact throughout.

1
Export each contact list to CSV

In Outlook: File, then Open and Export, then Import/Export. Select "Export to a file," choose Comma Separated Values, and select one contact folder at a time. Save each as a clearly named CSV file.

2
Merge into a single master file in Excel

Open all exported CSV files. Copy the data from each into one master spreadsheet, pasting below the previous list. Confirm column headers align across all files before combining.

3
Remove duplicates

Select the full data range. Go to the Data tab and click Remove Duplicates. Match on Email Address at minimum. If email isn't consistently populated, also match on First Name, Last Name, and Company.

4
Review near-duplicates manually

Sort by Last Name, then by Email Address, to surface near-duplicates not caught by exact matching — same person with two email addresses, or a name with a typo. Merge or delete as appropriate.

5
Re-import the cleaned master list

Save the final file as CSV. In Outlook: File, Open and Export, Import/Export, then "Import from another program or file," select CSV. Choose "Do not import duplicate items." Import into a new folder named Master Contacts.

6
Verify and archive originals

Spot-check the imported master list against the originals. Keep all original individual lists archived — do not delete — until confirmed complete and accurate.

Paola Corrales
